column1: TD 4 | The 11 am track is in and shows no big changes with the storm center staying west of our beaches. Hopefully the center stays far enough west to lower our surge potential, however a storm surge warning is now up from Aripeka north. Surge forecasts in these areas are 3-5', with 2-4' forecast for the rest of our coast.

A lot can still happen. Remember Ian was supposed to hit us and took a hard right at Fort Myers. I know this is a tropical depression and not a hurricane but still crazier things have happened. We all need to stay on alert.

yes! I was in an evacuation zone in St Pete with Ian. Traveled 4 hrs in hurricane traffic to get east to Winter Garden. St Pete faired well. Winter Garden not so much. Power out for 2 days. Heavy rains. The Winter Garden family members should have come to me in St Pete. lol! 😂 Be aware, just like Denis says! In the end anything can happen. I’ll never forget Irma, either. That was my worst, in Winter Haven. Others have been thru worse than Irma. ❤️ 🙏 🌴 🌧️ ☀️
I'm so glad you bought a generator. It'll miss us.
